name: Duke Alvarez
alt: Duke Alvarez in a sim racing workshop with wheel rims and force-feedback hardware
voice: Blunt, useful, allergic to wobbly pedal decks.
beat: Duke translates torque, pedals, cockpits, and ecosystem lock-in into buying orders that do not eat your wallet twice.
motto: If the brake deck moves, the lap time lies.
benchMove: He asks what flexes before he asks what you want to upgrade.
favoriteWarning: A stronger wheelbase only makes a weak frame tell the truth louder.
profile: Former track-day spreadsheet addict, current hardware realist. Duke likes direct drive, hates vague compatibility promises, and treats every cockpit as a load path with a seat attached.

name: Val Chen
alt: Val Chen in a flight and space sim controls workshop
voice: Precise, calm, quietly devastating about bad cable routing.
beat: Val owns yokes, sticks, HOSAS layouts, head tracking, USB topology, and the difference between immersion and expensive desk clutter.
motto: Bind for stress, route for sleep.
currentObsession: USB power and mounts as first-class cockpit parts.
benchMove: She turns cockpit fantasies into control maps you can actually remember.
favoriteWarning: Panels are dessert. Clean primary controls are dinner.
profile: Val is the calm systems brain in the room: flight deck, HOSAS, HOTAS, head tracking, and the boring cable plan that saves the entire weekend.

name: Nina Brooks
alt: Nina Brooks in a golf simulator room with measuring tools and launch-monitor alignment gear
voice: Warm, direct, will absolutely ask your ceiling height first.
beat: Nina turns launch monitors, mats, screens, projectors, and room dimensions into build sheets that survive real walls.
currentObsession: Projector throw, hitting width, and joints that still work next year.
benchMove: She starts with the ceiling, then works backward to the launch monitor.
favoriteWarning: A cheap mat saves money once and annoys you every swing after.
profile: Nina sells the private-club dream, then measures the garage. Launch monitor fit, mats, screens, projectors, and subscriptions all go through her room-first filter.

name: Mac Donovan
alt: Mac Donovan at a DIY motion and tactile feedback electronics bench
voice: Sardonic maker energy, useful wiring paranoia.
beat: Mac covers tactile feedback, motion rigs, USB hubs, power, EMI, and the small mistakes that make a cockpit act haunted.
motto: Temporary wiring becomes permanent faster than pride admits.
currentObsession: Haptics, power bricks, ferrite cores, and labeling the thing before Future You curses Past You.
benchMove: He makes the invisible failure points visible before the cart gets expensive.
favoriteWarning: Motion is cool. Clean power and sane software profiles are cooler at 1:00 a.m.
profile: Mac is the shop-floor maker brain: tactile feedback, motion, button boxes, cable discipline, and the little electrical choices that keep a build from ruining Saturday.

name: Gus Calder
alt: Gus Calder in a compact ship bridge simulator workspace
voice: Dry, patient, deeply amused by repurposed racing wheels.
beat: Gus handles marine sims, oddball controllers, panels, helms, throttles, and every build that starts with 'hear me out.'
motto: Marine sim hardware is a scavenger hunt with labels.
currentObsession: Bridge Command controls, DIY axes, and making borrowed hardware feel intentional.
benchMove: He treats every odd controller as a possible helm until the software refuses the axis.
favoriteWarning: If the sim will not expose an axis, premium hardware cannot negotiate with it.
profile: Gus is the niche-builder translator: bridge panels, repurposed wheels, throttles, DIY boards, and the patience required when the marine shelf is not as stocked as racing or flight.
